ucs.title: [LLM] Silence (Whisper Spider) CHM-borne loader: hh.exe spawning script/PowerShell stagers
ucs.description: Hunts Silence's signature initial-access-to-execution chain where a spearphished Compiled HTML Help (.chm) file is opened by hh.exe and detonates an embedded JS/VBS/PowerShell stager that retrieves Silence.Downloader / Truebot. Few benign products invoke hh.exe to launch interpreters, making this an extremely high-fidelity finger-print for this crew which CrowdStrike tracks as WHISPER SPIDER.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="hh.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval suspicious_args=if(match(process,"(?i)(-enc|-e |FromBase64String|DownloadString|DownloadFile|IEX|Invoke-Expression|New-Object\s+Net\.Web|http[s]?://|\\\\\\\\)"),1,0)
| join type=outer host [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as chm_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.chm" Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Outlook\\*","*\\Content.Outlook\\*","*\\INetCache\\*") by host Filesystem.dest
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` ]
| where suspicious_args=1 OR isnotnull(chm_files)
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process process process_name chm_files
ucs.kql:
let chmDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName endswith ".chm"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Content.Outlook\", @"\INetCache\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
| project ChmDropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ChmFile=FileName, ChmPath=FolderPath, ChmHash=SHA256, ChmInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let emailChm = EmailAttachmentInfo
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName endswith ".chm"
| project EmailTime=Timestamp, ChmHash=SHA256, Sender=SenderFromAddress, Recipient=RecipientEmailAddress, Subject;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "hh.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| extend SuspiciousArgs = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-enc\b|-e\s|FromBase64String|DownloadString|DownloadFile|IEX|Invoke-Expression|New-Object\s+Net\.Web|https?://|\\\\)"
| join kind=leftouter chmDrops on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter emailChm on ChmHash
| where SuspiciousArgs or isnotempty(ChmFile) or isnotempty(Sender)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ChmFile, ChmPath, Sender, Recipient, Subject
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Silence/Whisper Spider has used weaponized CHM files as their primary delivery vector against Russian/CIS banks since 2017 (Group-IB, Kaspersky, Cyber Forensicator reporting). The combination of (a) hh.exe as parent of an interpreter, (b) suspicious download/Base64 args, and (c) a CHM staged in Outlook/Temp/Downloads is a very specific Silence loader fingerprint and is rarely seen in legitimate en

ucs.title: [LLM] Silence lateral movement to banking processing hosts via PsExec + Atmosphere/Truebot beacon over non-standard ports
ucs.description: Hunts Silence's late-stage tradecraft: after harvesting credentials they use PsExec/service-creation to pivot to ATM switch, SWIFT/AWS-CBR (Russian Central Bank Automated Workstation Client), and card-processing hosts, while their implants beacon to C2 over non-standard ports (e.g. 8443, 444, 9999). Correlating PsExec to financial-host name patterns with non-standard outbound C2 from the same source yields a high-confidence Silence pivot signal.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","paexec.exe","csexec.exe") OR (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process="*sc*\\\\*create*binPath*") OR Processes.process="*\\\\ADMIN$\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.src Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rex field=process "\\\\\\\\(?<target_host>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+)(?:\\\\|\s)"
| eval is_fin_target=if(match(coalesce(target_host,dest),"(?i)(ATM|SWIFT|AWSCBR|AWS-?CBR|CARD|CARDPROC|RBS|BANK|HSM|TELLER|PROCSRV)"),1,0)
| where is_fin_target=1
| join type=inner src [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (444,4443,8443,9999,8081,1080,4444,5555) All_Traffic.action="allowed" NOT All_Traffic.dest_category="internal" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port
    | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
    | rename src as src dest as c2_ip dest_port as c2_port
    | stats values(c2_ip) as c2_ip values(c2_port) as c2_port by src ]
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table firstTime lastTime src target_host dest user process c2_ip c2_port
ucs.kql:
let finPivot = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName in~ ("psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","paexec.exe","csexec.exe"))
     or (FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("sc ", "create", "binPath"))
     or (ProcessCommandLine has @"\\ADMIN$\")
| extend TargetHost = extract(@"\\\\([A-Za-z0-9\-_]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend Target = coalesce(TargetHost, RemoteDeviceName, DeviceName)
| where Target matches regex @"(?i)(ATM|SWIFT|AWSCBR|AWS-?CBR|CARD|CARDPROC|RBS|BANK|HSM|TELLER|PROCSRV)"
| project PivotTime=Timestamp, SrcDevice=DeviceName, DeviceId, AccountName, Target, ProcessCommandLine;
let nonStdC2 = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where RemotePort in (444, 4443, 8443, 9999, 8081, 1080, 4444, 5555)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP)) and isnotempty(RemoteIP)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE","teams.exe")
| project C2Time=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let surveillance = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("BitBlt","GetDeviceCaps","CreateCompatibleBitmap","avicap32","VFW_")
     or FileName in~ ("silence.exe","atmosphere.exe","truebot.exe")
| project SurvTime=Timestamp, DeviceId;
finPivot
| join kind=inner nonStdC2 on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter surveillance on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', PivotTime, C2Time)) < 1440
| project PivotTime, SrcDevice, AccountName, Target, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteIP, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessFileName, SurveillancePresent=isnotempty(SurvTime)
| order by PivotTime desc
ucs.rationale: Silence's documented endgame in Russian/CIS bank intrusions is to reach the Russian Central Bank AWS-CBR workstation, ATM control servers, and card processing systems, then issue fraudulent transfers or jackpot ATMs (Group-IB 2017/2019, SecureList). They consistently use PsExec/paexec for the final pivot, run a SurveillanceModule that screen/video-records analyst workstations to learn the procedur
